sections.paragraph:
συνθλάω fut. συνθλάσω LXX; 1 aor. συνέθλασα LXX. Pass.: 1 fut. συνθλασθήσομαι; pf. ptc. acc. συντεθλασμένον Is 42:3 v.l. (Alexis Com. [IV b.c.] Fgm. 270, 3 Kock [in Athen. 11, 466e]; Eratosth. p. 13, 18; Diod S 2, 57, 2; Manetho, Apot.. 5, 201; Plut., Artox. 1021 [19, 9]; Michel 817, 21 [IV b.c.]; LXX) to crush in such a way that an object is put in pieces, crush (together), dash to pieces, pass., abs. (Aristot., Probl. 1, 38, 863b, 13) Mt 21:44 (RSwaeles, NTS 6, ’60, 310–13); Lk 20:18.—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

acc. acc. = accusative

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Alexis Alexis Com , IV/III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Eratosth Eratosth , III b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

abs. abs. = absolute

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 972
